description: Create the top-level init orchestrator (internal/boot/init.go) that wires together bootstrap, mode detection, mode execution, boot finalization, and exec /sbin/init -- matching the flow of the overlay/init shell script. Also port the utility functions (pinfo, perr, pfatal, reinit_debug, setup_kernel, cleanup) from overlay/libexec/k3os/functions. Update main.go to call this new init orchestrator instead of relying on /usr/init shell script handoff.
steps: Create internal/boot/init.go with an Init struct that holds all sub-phase runners (Bootstrap, ModeDetector, ModeRunner, Finalizer)

Write tests first in internal/boot/init_test.go: test that Init.Run() calls phases in correct order (bootstrap -> mode detect -> mode-specific -> boot finalize -> exec /sbin/init), test rescue/fallback on errors, test debug mode propagation from k3os.debug cmdline

Port utility functions from overlay/libexec/k3os/functions: pinfo/perr/pfatal map to slog.Info/slog.Error/slog.Error+os.Exit, reinit_debug checks /proc/cmdline for k3os.debug and enables debug logging, setup_kernel mounts kernel squashfs and bind-mounts modules/firmware, cleanup removes /run/k3os and writes mode file
Implement Init.Run() method that orchestrates the full boot sequence with error handling and rescue shell fallback

Update main.go initrd() function: after enterchroot.Mount() succeeds, the binary is now exec'd as /usr/init. Register a new reexec handler 'usr-init' (or modify the existing flow) so that when the binary runs as /usr/init inside the chroot, it calls boot.Init.Run() instead of a shell script

Ensure the enterchroot.Enter() -> run() function execs the binary itself (self-exec) as /usr/init rather than a shell script, OR configure enterchroot to exec a path that is a symlink to the k3os binary
Run golangci-lint run ./... and fix any issues
Run go test ./internal/boot/... and verify all tests pass
Run go test ./... to verify no regressions
Commit with message 'feat(init): wire up init orchestrator replacing shell-based init'
acceptance_criteria: Init.Run() calls bootstrap, mode detection, mode handler, finalizer, and execs /sbin/init in correct order
Error in any phase triggers rescue shell (exec bash) matching original shell behavior
Debug mode from k3os.debug cmdline enables slog debug level
main.go or enterchroot correctly invokes the Go-based init instead of shell /usr/init
findings: All 10 init orchestrator tests pass (table-driven subtests for each failure mode plus ordered phase test and cmdline error test). Init.Run() calls bootstrap, mode detection, mode registry, mode handler, finalizer, and exec /sbin/init in correct order. Error in any phase triggers rescue. Debug mode from k3os.debug cmdline enables slog debug level. main.go initrd() detects post-chroot via /.base sentinel and calls postChroot() which wires real dependencies (osimpl types, mode.Detector, modes.NewRegistry, bootstrap.Bootstrapper, finalize.Finalizer) into boot.Init struct. realProcessExecutor wraps syscall.PivotRoot and syscall.Exec. Helper functions readCmdline, blockProbe, statfsCheck, readVersionID, fsTypeName support the orchestrator. golangci-lint 0 issues. go test ./... all green. go build ./... clean.
